raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Governor Newsom announced a $200 million investment in the California Violence Intervention Program (CalVIP), marking the largest ever investment by any state in community violence intervention and prevention efforts.",
    "CalVIP provides competitive grants to cities and community-based organizations for services such as community education, diversion programs, outreach to at-risk transitional age youth, and violence reduction models.",
    "Communities that received CalVIP funding during the 2018 cycle saw homicides decrease by three times more than those that did not receive CalVIP support.",
    "In 2019, CalVIP-supported programs helped reduce gun homicides among the highest risk age group (15-29 years old) to the lowest rate since 1970.",
    "California suffered a 69 percent increase in gun homicides in the last three months of 2020 compared to 2019.",
    "January 2021 was the single deadliest month for gun homicides in California since 2007.",
    "Massachusetts invests just under $25 million in community violence intervention programs, despite having only 1/12 as many gun homicides as California.",
    "New York invests $20 million annually in community violence intervention programs while having 1/3 as many gun homicides as California."
  ]
}
```
